title: 沙特阿拉伯油轮被撞后油价突破97美元，特朗普升级伊朗威胁
content:
周四，油价继续攀升，布伦特原油期货突破 97 美元，此前有报道称，沙特阿拉伯沿海油轮遭受袭击，美国再次威胁要升级对伊朗的打击力度。

美国东部时间凌晨 3:45，7 月份交割的布伦特原油期货上涨 3.9%，至每桶 97.76 美元，创下 6 月份以来的最高水平3.美国西德克萨斯中质原油期货上涨约 3%，至每桶 89.50 美元。

英国海事贸易运营部周四在社交媒体平台 Xon 上发布消息称，一艘油轮在 Al Shuqaiq 西南约 70 海里处被击中。这次袭击引发了船上起火，船员正在扑救，但没有人员伤亡的报告。

也门胡塞武装声称声称，他们用无人机和导弹瞄准了两艘沙特阿拉伯油轮，据称这艘油轮违反了海上封锁。

CNBC 无法独立核实这些袭击事件。如果得到证实，这将是自伊朗支持的激进组织宣布对沙特阿拉伯实施海上封锁以来的首次袭击。

在特朗普警告称，每当德黑兰在霍尔木兹海峡袭击一艘船只时，美国就会摧毁伊朗的一座桥梁或发电厂，这标志着美伊停火破裂后紧张局势进一步升级。特朗普表示，无论是通过导弹、火箭、无人机还是任何其他装置或武器袭击霍尔木兹海峡的一艘船只，美国都将轰炸并摧毁一座桥梁或发电厂。

伊朗回应称，如果华盛顿实施这些袭击，它将对该地区与美国相关的基础设施和能源资产进行报复。

“如果美国人瞄准伊朗的一座桥梁或一座发电厂，伊朗将会，一位不愿透露姓名的伊朗军方消息人士告诉国营塔斯尼姆通讯社。

周四，前美国驻沙特阿拉伯大使约瑟夫·韦斯特法尔 (Joseph Westphal) 在接受 CNBC 的“访问中东”节目时表示，“很难准确了解这一切的走向。”

“没有真正的战略，”他说。 “战争部长一直在国会作证，要求为战争提供更多资金。国会对此非常非常焦虑。他们认为没有足够的战略和计划来提供这些额外资金。”

虽然美国花费了“巨额资金”，但韦斯特法尔表示，华盛顿没有资源继续与伊朗的战争，也没有保护通过红海的航运。

“我们遇到了一个真正的问题。在这里，”他说。 “随着天然气价格持续上涨，其他大宗商品价格因此持续上涨，这将引起美国公众的反应，不仅反对这场战争，也反对正在推行的战略，而这个国家的任何人都几乎不理解这一点。”周三，国务卿马可·鲁比奥表示，伊朗并没有“认真”与华盛顿达成协议，同时坚称美国仍然“致力于外交”汇丰银行在周三晚间的一份报告中表示，近期油价上涨反映了美伊停火破裂后对霍尔木兹海峡的新担忧，并警告说，前景现在取决于外交能否恢复可预测的航运流量。

“自 7 月 7 日至 8 日以来，由于伊朗对经过霍尔木兹海峡的船只发动袭击，促使美国采取报复行动，停火协议已经破裂。该银行高级全球石油和天然气分析师金·福斯蒂尔 (Kim Fustier) 表示。

“核心问题仍未解决：是否管理通行以及由谁管理。事后看来，通过美国管理的阿曼通道的交通量增加得越多，这种结果就越不适合伊朗，”富斯蒂尔补充道。

title: 随着伊朗冲突扩大，胡塞武装声称在红海袭击了沙特阿拉伯油轮
content:
德黑兰支持的胡塞武装声称在红海袭击了两艘沙特阿拉伯油轮，威胁要在美国完成对伊朗连续 12 晚的袭击后在中东冲突中开辟一条新战线。

也门胡塞武装周三表示，他们用无人机和导弹袭击了两艘沙特阿拉伯油轮，名为恩塞利亚 (Encelia) 和莱拉 (Layla)，涉嫌侵犯其海上安全。

此次袭击如果得到证实，将是该武装组织宣布对沙特阿拉伯实施全面封锁以来的首次袭击。

国营沙特通讯社 (SPA) 证实，恩塞利亚号在穿越红海时遭到攻击，导致船头起火。该机构援引运输总局一位未透露姓名的官员的话说，所有船员都很安全，并将这次袭击描述为违反国际法的行为。

SPA 没有提供有关 Layla 油轮的最新信息。

另外，英国海事贸易运营部 (UKMTO) 周三报告称，一枚“未知射弹”击中了距离沙特沿海城镇 Al Shuqaiq 约 130 公里的一艘沙特阿拉伯油轮。阿拉伯。 UKMTO表示，该事件导致船上起火，没有人员伤亡。

美国中央司令部表示，美国军队昨晚完成了对伊朗的另一轮打击，打击了包括海上能力、导弹和无人机储存设施以及防空资产在内的军事目标。

中央司令部在一份声明中表示，“这次袭击进一步削弱了伊朗攻击民用海员和商船的能力。”

它补充说，美军已瞄准了数十艘伊朗船只。伊朗本月在陆地上建立军事基地，同时恢复对伊斯兰共和国的海上封锁。截至周四晚间，中央司令部表示，已对 9 艘商船进行了重定向，并禁用了 1 艘，以阻止船只进出伊朗港口。

总部位于华盛顿的智库战争研究所在其最新更新中表示，鉴于胡塞武装对沙特阿拉伯港口的封锁，至少有 7 艘船只改变了航向，以避免通过曼德海峡。曼德海峡是连接红海、亚丁湾和全球市场的商船交通的咽喉要道。

ISW 表示，美国也越来越多地袭击伊朗南部以外的伊朗目标，包括伊朗西部和西北部。

随着美国和伊朗的敌对行动加剧，周四上午油价走高，延续了近期涨幅。

国际基准布伦特原油9 月交割的原油期货最后一次上涨 3.1%，至每桶 97.06 美元。与此同时，9 月交割的美国西德克萨斯中质原油期货上涨 2.3%，至 88.86 美元。

美国总统唐纳德·特朗普警告称，每次伊朗袭击具有战略意义的霍尔木兹海峡的船只，美国都会摧毁伊朗的一座桥梁或发电厂，这标志着双方上个月签署的临时和平协议进一步破裂。

“从现在起，任何时候伊朗伊斯兰共和国都将摧毁伊朗的桥梁或发电厂。特朗普周三在真理社会帖子中表示：“如果在霍尔木兹海峡向一艘船只开枪，无论是使用导弹、火箭、无人机还是任何其他装置或武器，美国都会轰炸并摧毁一座桥梁或发电厂。”

作为回应，伊朗表示将对该地区与美国相关的基础设施和能源资产进行攻击，以报复这些袭击。

title: 谷歌因人工智能成本不断上升而烧钱
content: 图片来源，路透社 图片说明，谷歌及其母公司 Alphabet 在 AI 上投入巨资 作者：Kali HaysTechnology 记者 2026 年 7 月 22 日发布 47 分钟前更新 谷歌母公司 Alphabet 近几个月来其业务持续增长，但人工智能 (AI) 基础设施支出不断增长，使其剩余现金陷入负值。该公司的自由现金流（支付运营和投资后维持的现金）为负 59 亿美元根据过去的财务记录，这是至少十年来的首次（43 亿英镑）。Alphabet 今年在人工智能方面的支出预计将达到 2050 亿美元，高于 1900 亿美元，主要科技公司竞相围绕新一波技术进行开发。与此同时，Alphabet 的季度总收入达到 1198 亿美元，与去年同期相比增长 23%。但该公司股价在 2019 年下跌了 4%。盘后交易中。谷歌首席财务官阿纳特·阿什卡纳齐(Anat Ashkanazi)在与财务分析师的电话会议上指出，由于资本支出不断增长，该公司自由现金流呈现负值，而这些支出基本上都与人工智能支出有关。她表示，该公司第二季度支出为450亿美元，其中60%的成本用于服务器，其余40%用于数据中心。Alphabet今年第一季度的资本支出为360亿美元。阿什卡纳齐在电话会议上表示，谈到人工智能，“需求仍然超过投资”。“只要我们看到这些有吸引力的投资机会，我们就会继续投资。”谷歌首席执行官桑达尔·皮查伊(Sundar Pichai)表示，向人工智能工具和能力的技术转变仍然“感觉像是跨多个领域转变的早期阶段”，该公司围绕其支出产生财务回报的计划是“有纪律的”。“就我所看到的前沿能力而言，仍有很多工作要做财富管理公司 Killik & Co 的合伙人雷切尔·温特 (Rachel Winter) 表示，投资者对 Google 的支出感到有些惊讶。他们表示，今年的支出总额将在 1950 亿美元至 2050 亿美元之间，所以这些数字很大，而且我认为，业绩公布后，股价在盘后交易中下跌了约 3.5%，这表明有一点。埃隆·马斯克(Elon Musk)控制的电动汽车公司特斯拉周三也报告称，由于自身投资成本不断增加，第二季度自由现金流为负11亿美元。根据其财务记录，这是该公司两年来首次出现剩余现金负数。特斯拉首席财务官瓦伊巴夫·塔内贾(Vaibhav Taneja)在与分析师的电话会议上表示，该公司今年将支出高达250亿美元，是其资本支出的两倍多。 2025年。他补充说，特斯拉正处于“一个大投资周期”，未来三年其支出可能会进一步增加。特斯拉的股价在盘后交易中也下跌了4%。相关主题谷歌Alphabet人工智能更多关于这个故事亚马逊股价下跌，因为它加入了大型科技人工智能支出热潮发布于2月6日

title: 被流氓 OpenAI 模型黑客攻击的公司称这是“一个警钟”
content: 图片来源，Bloomberg via Getty Images 图片说明，Hugging Face 联合创始人兼首席科学官 Thomas Wolf 作者：Osmond Chia 商业记者发布时间 2026 年 7 月 23 日，英国夏令时间 06:07 更新于 1 小时前 Hugging Face 是一家科技初创公司，在 OpenAI 一些最先进的人工智能 (AI) 模型失控后遭到黑客攻击，周四表示，该事件是“一起托马斯·沃尔夫（Thomas Wolf）告诉 BBC 新闻日广播节目，“这将是我们看到的最常见的网络攻击类型之一”，但大多数公司并没有意识到“游戏规则已经改变”。BBC 已联系 OpenAI 征求评论。ChatGPT 制造商周二表示，其人工智能模型在试验期间突破了安全测试环境，发起了网络攻击。该公司表示，该事件“史无前例”，正在与 Hugging Face 进行调查。AI 代理能够在人类指令后单独操作完成任务。Wolf 表示，当 7 月中旬迹象浮出水面时，Hugging Face 最初并不知道攻击起源于何处，但该公司能够遏制这一漏洞。Hugging Face 是世界上最大的共享 AI 模型的开源中心之一，经常被技术开发人员和研究人员使用。Wolf 表示，此次漏洞与Hugging Face 经常面临的常见网络攻击，OpenAI 很快通知该公司，其模型是黑客攻击的幕后黑手。身兼该公司首席科学官的 Wolf 表示，在“非常短的时间内”，Hugging Face 的网络遭受了 17,000 次来自各种 IP（互联网协议）地址的攻击。Wolf 表示，这是对其他公司的警告，他们必须加强网络安全防御，以应对此类攻击。这次黑客攻击“令人担忧”，因为它表明 OpenAI 的模型忽略了典型的网络攻击。机器智能研究所的内特·苏亚雷斯 (Nate Soares) 表示，“从某种意义上说，它知道这不是创造者的本意。它只是不在乎。”什么是人工智能，它是如何工作的，为什么有些人担心它？发布于 2025 年 7 月 29 日，你的工作会被人工智能取代吗？以下是受影响最严重的角色 1天前发布 其他组织也注意到了这一事件。英国政府发言人表示，该国人工智能安全研究所正在研究人工智能系统在该事件中的表现，并将继续与OpenAI和其他实验室合作，加强保障措施。他们敦促各组织采取注册政府支持的Cyber Essentials认证计划等措施，加强网络安全措施。该事件发生在该行业的关键时刻，此前美国政府上个月责令美国科技公司出于国家安全考虑，Anthropic 将限制对其人工智能模型的访问。几周后商务部解除了限制。业内人士还对中国广泛使用开源模型提出安全担忧，允许任何人安装和定制主要开发商发布的人工智能工具。中国初创公司Moonshot AI将于7月27日发布其Kimi K3开源模型。自上周首次亮相以来，它引起了业界的关注，许多人将其视为领先的西方人工智能系统的有力竞争对手。但周三，一名白宫顾问指责 Moonshot 正在“大规模”窃取美国顶级人工智能模型的能力。相关主题国际商业人工智能网络安全更多有关此故事的谷歌因人工智能成本螺旋式上升而烧钱的文章47分钟前发布什么是克劳德神话以及它会带来什么风险？4月17日发布

title: 新首相安迪·伯纳姆承诺“新经济模式”，对冲基金关注英国股市
content:
Hedge fund short-sellers have ramped up bets against U.K.-listed stocks this year, with disclosed positions surging fivefold in the first half of 2026.

Now, investors believe a policy blitz under newPrime Minister Andy Burnhamcould widen the opportunity set across U.K. equities on both the long and short sides of their portfolios.

Burnham took office this week, promising to lead a "cost-of-living government" focused on tackling rising living expenses. In his inaugural speech on Monday, he pledged a "new economic model" for Britain, including a 10-year plan to reindustrialize the country, with housing costs and utilities affordability emerging as early key pillars of his agenda.

Hedge funds say a domestic policy shake-up could fuel a raft of long and short ideas across U.K. sectors. That includes conviction bets against squeezed sectors and companies, and relative value trades that involve buying companies expected to benefit from Burnham's policies while shorting weaker businesses in the same industry.

"Where we are today is that there are lots of winners and losers, which is good because we want that dispersion," said Alyx Wood, chief investment officer of Kernow Asset Management. "There's an explosive cocktail of really interesting things going on right now. It's pretty fast moving."

Short-selling — a core component of many hedge fund strategies, in which investors bet against a stock or other security intending to profit from its drop in value — has returned with a vengeance to the U.K. market this year.

The number of U.K. companies with aggregate disclosed short positions of at least 5% of their shares surged to 27 in the first half of 2026, according to an analysis by law firm White & Case.

That's a sharp rise from just five in the same period last year.

"The U.K.'s leadership change is creating a degree of continued uncertainty and volatility about policy across key sectors including energy, utilities, transport and housebuilding," said Patrick Sarch, White & Case's head of U.K. public M&A.

"As a new policy platform coalesces following new governmental appointments, policies will be proposed, markets will react, and there will be a moderation and prioritization process to determine what is feasible and in what timeframe. We expect to see a relatively protracted period of increased uncertainty for price discovery, creating additional opportunities for short-selling."

Burnham on Tuesday announced plans to remove sales tax from household electricity to provide "breathing space" for customers facing cost-of-living pressures, putting energy costs and utility affordability into sharper political focus, investors say.

Wood said Kernow is bearish on U.K. utilities, highlighting high leverage at individual companies, regulatory, operational and licensing pressures, and the vast investment required in Britain's water and power infrastructure.

"Normally utilities, in dangerous times, are your safe bets. Utilities are probably where we are most negative," Wood told CNBC, citing substandard U.K. water infrastructure, unmet consumer expectations, and regulatory uncertainty that has intensified political scrutiny.

Burnham also wants to build more public housing and pledged Monday to end "rough sleeping" — or unhoused people living on the street — in the U.K. Investors say a policy overhaul aimed at tackling Britain's housing affordability crisis could create winners and losers in the homebuilding sector.

HomebuilderVistry Groupand construction materials companyIbstockwere among the most heavily shorted U.K. companies in the first half, White & Case research shows, with aggregate short positions of almost 16% and 13%, respectively.

Wood said Kernow is short Vistry, noting its debt accumulation, and longBerkeley Group, which he described as a winner because of its stronger balance sheet and better management of planning applications. Others, such asGalliford Try, could benefit from a pick-up in affordable housing construction, he added.

"Most U.K. housebuilders trade at absurd discounts to economic reality. Quite often, near-term profits comfortably exceed the market capitalization," Wood said in a recent note.

Investors have followed Burnham'spath to 10 Downing Streetwith some consternation, amidfearsof a leftward lurch by Labour and a more fiscally loose approach to public spending. But his surprise pick for finance minister,John Healey, has gone some way to calming markets, with the former defense secretary broadly regarded as a "safe pair of hands."

White & Case's Sarch said the evolving policy backdrop could sustain relative value opportunities across sectors.

"We expect investors to look beyond company-specific short positions and for there to be considerable portfolio-based long-short strategies, taking both long and short positions across sectors according to which they believe stand to benefit from, and be challenged by, the new policy agenda," he added.

Edgar Allen, founder and chief investment officer of High Ground Investment Management, said housebuilders — along with certain other sectors, such as banks — could face further taxes, although he added that this prospect was already reflected in lower valuations across those sectors.

Allen said Burnham — who replaces Labour Party leader Keir Starmer, whoresigned last month— inherits record government spending, high debts, an "unsustainable" deficit and gilt yields "far above anything Liz Truss managed."

"It's easy to see why investor, consumer and business confidence are all at multi-year lows," Allen told CNBC via email.

Still, Allen pointed to signs of improvement, including economic growth and rising productivity, which underpin what he called "real value" in the U.K.

"We expect to see further takeover activity as foreign companies pay record premia for U.K. stocks while still getting bargains," he added.

title: 白银和黄金价格谨慎反弹，但分析师认为持续反弹的希望渺茫
content:
贵金属在经历了一段持续的抛售压力后，最近几天有所反弹，但分析师表示，黄金和白银价格可能会面临重返今年早些时候创下的历史高点的艰难道路。

现货白银周三早盘交易价为每盎司 59.47 美元（美国东部时间上午 6:33），较上周末的 55.9 美元/盎司上涨约 6.3%。黄金同期上涨约 2.4%，至 4,119.04 美元/盎司。

在周三的一份报告中，ING 大宗商品策略师 Warren Patterson 和 Ewa Manthey 将涨幅归因于“近期疲软后逢低吸纳”，而不是“地缘政治或宏观经济背景的重大转变”。

在经历了全年及以后的强劲反弹后，这两种贵金属仍远低于今年早些时候创下的历史高点。 2025 年。两者均在 1 月底创下历史新高，当时现货黄金触及 5,589.38 美元/盎司，白银触及 121.67 美元/盎司。

利率上升和美元走强令贵金属黯然失色，因为伊朗战争带来的油价上涨已将市场动态转移到其他地方。

“虽然中东的紧张局势仍然对贵金属构成支撑，但市场正在衡量美国经济疲软的情况。 Patterson 和 Manthey 说道。

ING 分析师补充说，黄金“可能对能源市场的发展和美国货币政策的预期保持敏感”，但表示，如果工业金属持续走强以及避险需求，白银的表现可能会继续跑赢大盘。相比之下，美国银行分析师认为，在截至 6 月底的三个月中录得 13 年来最差季度表现后，金价有可能进一步恶化。

美国银行在 7 月 16 日的一份报告中表示，“死亡交叉信号、净多头头寸上升以及与主要峰值的相似性增加了更长时间、更深层次回调的风险。”

当股票短期波动时，就会出现“死亡交叉”模式。移动平均线（通常计算超过 50 天）低于其长期移动平均线（通常计算超过 200 天）。

瑞银对白银反弹的潜力持怀疑态度，并警告投资者不要开始增加对白银的投资。

瑞士银行本周将白银有吸引力的切入点的价格目标从 55 美元/盎司左右下调至 48-50 美元/盎司的范围。

“我们认为短期内白银价格会上涨。”由于中东紧张局势升级、机会成本上升以及美元坚挺继续打压投资者情绪，白银的逆风可能持续存在，”瑞银策略师多米尼克·施奈德(Dominic Schnider)在7月20日的一份报告中写道。 “白银面临自上而下的背景，投资者几乎没有动力增加多头头寸。由于投资需求参差不齐，白银价格尚未找到坚实的底部。”

但美国黄金和白银开发商 Hycroft Mining 执行董事长兼首席执行官黛安·加勒特 (Diane Garrett) 周二向 CNBC 的“Squawk Box Europe”表示，最近的价格恶化是“正常的调整”，并补充说“这不是一次破碎的牛市”市场。”

“大宗商品的基本面仍然极其强劲，尤其是黄金，因为它已经超越美国国债成为第一大资产类别，并且它正在……成为金融体系的架构，”她说。

“人们不想拥有由其他国家债务支持的硬资产，而且我们看到央行连续 17 个月买入，这些数字非常引人注目。”

“对于白银来说。同样，因为它不仅是一种货币金属，也是一种工业金属，它正在推动人工智能革命和超级计算机——所有这些都需要白银来实现，而且没有替代品。”

——CNBC 的 Joe Wilkins 也为本报告做出了贡献。

title: 印度“蟑螂党”领导的Z世代抗议活动正在考验莫迪的领导力
content:
Thousands of Gen Z student protestors, led by the Cockroach Janta Party, or CJP, a pseudo-political outfit, continued to gather in New Delhi on Wednesday even as authorities have cracked down on the movement.

India has one of theworld's largest youth populations, and the protests are the result of Gen Z anger stemming from youth unemployment and lack of economic mobility, experts said.

"The ongoing demonstrations could represent the most significant public challenge to the government since the farmers' mobilization in 2020-2021," Atman Trivedi, partner at Washington-based DGA Albright Stonebridge Group, told CNBC.

The CJP started as an "online political satire" but has now turned into a "serious, real-world protest movement," Trivedi added.

The student protest started on June 6,demanding the resignationof India's Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan, blaming him for the exam paper leaks that happened in May. It elicited support from Sonam Wangchuk, an activist and educator, who has been on ahunger strike for 25 daysand is receiving medical care.

Demonstrations intensified this week after students on Monday breached security barricades surrounding the protest site as they marched toward the parliament andwere tear-gassed and beaten up by Delhi Police. The police have said that more than100 personnel were injuredin "violent protests."

It seemed that police used violent means to "cause maximum damage possible" rather than to disperse the crowd, Lavanya Singh, who was present at the protests on July 20, told CNBC. The 26-year-old returned to the protest on Wednesday and plans to attend it "every day."

"The [exam paper] leak, is an example of how this govt has repeatedly failed us," Singh said, adding that a mixture of "feeling betrayed, anger, and helplessness" is driving the protestors. The CJP is not "perfect" but is trying to deliver justice and is listening to the youth of our nation, Singh said.

The Indian home ministry did not respond to CNBC's request for comment.

The CJP was launched on May 16 by political communications strategist and Boston University student Abhijeet Dipke, after Chief Justice of India Surya Kant referred to peopleattacking institutionsas "parasites" and likened some unemployed youngsters and activists to "cockroaches" during a courtroom hearing. The CJI later said hisoral observations were "misquoted."

The CJP has since gone viral on social media and amassed more than 22 million followers, but the account wastaken down on Wednesday, with Dipke blaming Prime Minister Narendra Modi in a post on X.

Modi's political rivals are also mounting pressure on the government to respond to the demands of the students and are seeking action against those who assaulted the protestors.

Rahul Gandhi, India's leader of opposition, who was briefly detained on Tuesday for holding a protest in front of the prime minister's residence in solidarity with the students, claimed that roughly 152 exam paper leaks have occurred over the last 10 years,affecting more than 75 millionstudents.

The ruling Bharatiya Janata Party has said the governmentwill investigate Gandhi'sclaims, who said that "The Indian education system has been rigged" in a press conference on Wednesday.

In May, the National Eligibility cum Entrance Test, a crucial exam for admission to medical colleges, wascancelled due to allegations of a paper leak, affecting millions of students. A retest was conducted in June.

The country's education minister, in a post on X on Wednesday, said the "government remains 100% committed to discussing" the exam paper leak matter and "addressing every genuine concern of our youth on the floor of the House."

The students of India deserve far better than "being treated as props in a political campaign," and they "deserve certainty over chaos, solutions over slogans and responsibility over disruption."

The CJP has said the protestsare spreading across multiple citiesin India, saying that the "nation has awakened."

Angad Chabra, a 17-year-old student protesting in India's tech hub of Bengaluru, who is set to start his engineering degree, said frustration among students was "too high to stay silent any longer" and that CJP had provided a platform for youths to rally together.

India ranks as the "highest-risk country" in the Verisk Maplecroft's Civil Unrest Index, a global risk analysis firm. Grievances are increasing in size, and average protest participation has more than doubled over the past year, while unrest is now geographically dispersed across India, Reema Bhattacharya, head of Asia research at Verisk Maplecroft, said in a note on Wednesday.

"After a decade in office, weak employment outcomes are becoming harder to attribute solely to legacy factors, feeding the strongest anti-incumbency narrative seen in his 12 years in power, ahead of the 2029 election," she added.

The CJP protest has also galvanized support from various regional political parties and even from farmers' groups. Prominent farmers' union leader Rakesh Tikaitpledged his supportfor the students' protest.

The CJP is serving as a lightning rod for almost anyone with a grievance against the government, Pramit Pal Chaudhuri, South Asia practice head at Eurasia Group, told CNBC, but warned that this was also making the movement "increasingly chaotic."

title: 特朗普技术顾问称，中国的 Moonshot AI 窃取了 Anthropic 的技术
content: 图片来源，Future Publishing via Getty Images 图片说明，Kimi K3 是由北京 Moonshot AI 开发的人工智能模型，作者：Osmond Chia 商务记者 发布时间：2026 年 7 月 23 日，英国夏令时 01:52 6 小时前更新 白宫顾问指责中国 Moonshot AI 正在“大规模”窃取美国顶级人工智能 (AI) 模型的能力。美国总统唐纳德·特朗普的科技顾问迈克尔Kratsios 表示，Moonshot AI 通过所谓的蒸馏（即较弱的人工智能模型从更强的人工智能模型中提取答案）来开展这项活动。Kratsios 周三在外部社交帖子中表示，Moonshot 还获得了访问受限制的 Nvidia 尖端服务器来训练其模型的权限。BBC 已联系 Moonshot、中国驻华盛顿大使馆、Anthropic、白宫和 Nvidia 征求评论。Kratsios 在 X 上表示，美国政府掌握了 Moonshot AI 的信息。从Anthropic的Fable AI中“提炼”出的能力用于开发其K3模型。Kimi K3上周推出后受到了全世界的关注，许多人认为它缩小了西方和中国人工智能模型之间的差距。 Moonshot 表示，其 K3 型号能够与美国顶尖技术相媲美。Kratsios 表示，Moonshot 很可能还使用了由 Nvidia GB300 Grace Blackwell 计算平台提供支持的服务器。由于担心 Nvidia 最先进的芯片可能被中国军方使用，华盛顿在 2022 年限制了这些芯片的出口。从那时起，世界各地的政府都严厉打击了芯片的走私活动。Kratsios 的指控是在财政部长斯科特提出的一天后提出的。贝森特周二表示，美国将审查中国人工智能模型是否窃取了美国竞争对手的能力。周三，贝森特还表示，当中国企业通过进行“工业规模的蒸馏攻击”而“越过界限”盗窃知识产权时，制裁“将摆在桌面上”。贝森特在社交媒体上表示，“我们支持开源人工智能及其所释放的创新。但开源并不是美国知识产权的开放季节”。中国政府尚未发表公开声明回应Anthropic对最新指控表示不满，但此前曾表示，中国人工智能的发展是“自身奉献和努力以及国际合作的结果”。美国对中国人工智能公司加强审查之际，特朗普预计将于9月会见中国国家主席习近平。Moonshot AI将于7月27日发布Kimi K3作为开源模型，使其成为首个可供开发者和公众免费下载和定制的规模化模型。6月，Anthropic指责中国电子商务和科技巨头阿里巴巴利用蒸馏攻击“非法”提取其克劳德人工智能模型的能力。Anthropic敦促国会惩罚涉嫌攻击的公司，并加强措施防止美国技术被窃取。白宫四月份表示，将与美国人工智能公司更密切地合作，打击外国竞争对手窃取技术的“工业规模活动”。Kratsios在当时的一份备忘录中表示，这些活动旨在“系统性地破坏美国的研发并获取专有技术”相关话题国际商业人工智能中国白宫更多关于这个故事的OpenAI称其人工智能变得流氓并发起了“史无前例”的网络攻击发布20小时前中国的Moonshot AI声称Kimi K3可以与OpenAI和Anthropic相媲美发布5天前

title: 人工智能支出盖过了增长，Alphabet 和特斯拉考验华尔街的耐心
content:
WhenAlphabetandTeslakicked off tech earnings season on Wednesday, one theme became immediately clear: AI spending is under a microscope.

Both companies reported negative free cash flow for the latest quarter and told investors to prepare for higher capital expenditures. They both also reported better-than-expected revenue, but that wasn't enough to prevent an after-market selloff, with Tesla shares sliding 4% and Alphabet down more than 3%.

It's a potentially ominous sign for the tech industry, particularly the other megacaps, which are mostly set to report quarterly results next week.MetaandMicrosoftare scheduled to report next Wednesday, followed a day later byAmazonandApple.

Much of the AI boom to date has been fueled by historic levels of infrastructure spending among a small crop of companies, including hefty investments into model developers OpenAI and Anthropic. But the recent emergence of cheaperopen-source models, largely out of China, along with signs that corporate America is gettingmore frugalwhen it comes to spending on AI services, has raised concerns about the future returns on investment.

Heading into Wednesday's reports, Alphabet's stock was already on pace for its third straight monthly decline after surging in April, while Tesla shares were down 11% in July and 17% for the year. The tech-heavy Nasdaq has dropped about 5% since reaching a record in early June.

While Alphabet and Tesla are both spending at unprecedented levels, their numbers vary dramatically.

Google's parent companyforecastcapex for this year of $195 billion to $205 billion and warned of higher numbers in 2027. Prior guidance was for spending of $180 billion to $190 billion. At the top end of the new range, Alphabet could be the biggest spender in tech this year, as Amazon'slatest guidancewas for more than $200 billion, though that number may increase when the company reports results next week.

Google and its hyperscaler peers are building out data centers packed with advanced chips so they can provide the computing power necessary to build and run the leading AI models and the services they power.

Mizuho analysts wrote in a note that Google's capex increase was "broadly anticipated," and that the overall story is positive, largely due to the surge in cloud revenue, which jumped 82% from a year earlier, blowing past estimates. Cloud margins expanded and usage of Google's Gemini model accelerated.

"As such we are surprised the stock is trading off after hours and would expect it to recover in trading tomorrow," wrote the analysts, who recommend buying the stock.

Teslareiteratedexpectations for more than $25 billion in capex this year, which would represent about 200% year-over-year growth. In the second quarter, capex soared 142% to $5.79 billion. The company boosted spending on self-driving technology, AI and robotics initiatives that CEOElon Muskhas been touting for years.

Tesla is now retooling its factories to make the two-seater driverless Cybercab, and to manufacture Optimus humanoid robots, which are still being developed, while also preparing to start construction of a sprawling AI chip-manufacturing plant in Texas.

"We should be spending on capex as fast as we can spend, as fast as we can without it being too wasteful," Musk said on the earnings call. He added, "It's ok to be a little less capital efficient if we get things done sooner."

For both companies, the aggressive growth plans are resulting in a major hit to their cash holdings.

Free cash flow at Tesla turned negative in the quarter, with a deficit of $1.1 billion after the company generated $146 million in free cash flow a year ago and $1.44 billion in the first quarter of 2026.

"This is a massive capex year but we are confident that all the things that we are investing in will yield incredible returns," Musk said. He compared Tesla's spending and building in "many different arenas simultaneously," to that of Henry Ford with the Model T.

"I think probably this is the fastest industrial scale-up since World War II in America," Musk said.

The numbers at Alphabet were even more stark, with free cash flow sinking to negative $5.9 billion after the company, which is lauded for its fat margins from online ads, generated almost $25 billion in free cash flow a year ago.

"We expect the free cash flow will remain under pressure, driven by our investments in technical infrastructure, which enables us to capitalize on the AI opportunity and continue to drive attractive returns," CFO Anat Ashkenazi said on the earnings call.

Most of the company's $44.9 billion in capex in the second quarter went to infrastructure to support the AI buildout, Ashkenazi said.

In addition to building its own data centers, Google executives said they also plan to rely on capacity from third-party cloud providersto meet feverish computing demand, building on a recent compute deal with Musk'sSpaceX, which now owns xAI and its Memphis data centers.

The results on Wednesday did nothing to squash the enthusiasm of bullish analysts and investors.

Keith Fitz-Gerald, principal at investment consulting firm Fitz-Gerald Group, said that at Tesla, "profitability is being sacrificed for infrastructure" just as it was previously at companies including Amazon andNetflix.

"I expect it to pay off in spades over the next 12-24, even 36 months," Fitz-Gerald wrote in a note after the report.

And Rebecca Wettemann, CEO of tech research firm Valoir, said in an email that Google's core business remains strong and that its AI investments are generating returns.

"Google's momentum should calm some market fears about AI overspending," she wrote. "Strong performance across its businesses show search isn't dead, advertising still matters, and cloud investment is still a good bet."

title: 由于燃油费用上涨，西南航空第三季度预测未达预期
content:
西南航空报告称，由于票价上涨越来越多地帮助该公司支付燃油费用，第二季度利润增长了 9% 以上，但其夏季前景低于华尔街预测。

该航空公司预计第三季度调整后收益将在 50 美分至 75 美分之间，低于分析师预期的 82 美分，尽管该公司预计销售额将比上年增长 17.5% 至 19.5%早些时候。达拉斯航空公司表示，与 2025 年第三季度相比，计划最多缩减 1% 的运力或保持持平。

西南航空预计全年调整后每股收益为 3.25 美元至 4.25 美元。 1 月份，该公司预测调整后的 2026 年全年每股收益至少为 4 美元。

虽然燃油价格因伊朗战争引发的创纪录飙升而有所回落，但成本一直不稳定，航空公司本月表示，他们今年基本上维持票价上涨。西南航空的平均单程票价从一年前的 186.65 美元上涨了近 21%，达到 225.61 美元。

首席财务官汤姆·多克西 (Tom Doxey) 周三对 CNBC 表示：“需求环境仍然非常强劲，其中包括国内航班。”

西南航空在过去两年中打破了其数十年的商业模式，以增加收入。它于 1 月份取消了开放座位，推出了基本经济舱票价，甚至停止了允许乘客免费托运两件行李的长期政策。

Doxey 表示，最近对飞机和设施的改进有助于吸引更多商务旅客。

第二季度，西南航空的收入增长了 16.4%，达到 84 亿美元。但西南航空的成本也大幅上升，第二季度的燃油费用较去年同期增加了 67%，达到 22.2 亿美元。

净利润增长 9.4%，达到 2.33 亿美元，即每股 47 美分，而去年同期为 2.13 亿美元，即每股 39 美分。

以下是西南航空第二季度的报告，与华尔街的预期相比，这是根据一致估计LSEG：不包括一次性项目，西南航空报告调整后每股收益为 94 美分，其中包括对兑换航班积分数量高于预期的客户进行的调整。西南航空改变了旧政策，对航班积分设置了过期日期，从 2025 年中期开始销售的许多舱位机票开始。因此，该公司的收入数据也进行了调整。

澄清：本文已更新，以反映调整后的收入与分析师的预期相当。

title: 机场送机费上涨了三分之一 - 这是最贵的
content: 图片来源，Universal Images Group via Getty Images 图片说明，盖特威克机场和斯坦斯特德机场被发现收取英国主要机场中最高的送机费 作者：Katy AustinTransport 通讯员 11 分钟前发布 最新分析发现，自去年夏天以来，英国主要机场的送机费平均上涨了三分之一。RAC 研究了在 20 个最繁忙的机场中距离航站楼最近的地点送机的成本机场 - 自去年以来已有 16 个机场收取了费用。伦敦的两个机场，盖特威克机场和斯坦斯特德机场，以 10 英镑的价格位居榜首。贸易机构英国机场协会表示，提供免费送机服务，收费是机场商业模式和气候变化目标的一部分。伦敦盖特威克机场是最高的“告别费”，在其任一航站楼外的前院收取 10 英镑/10 分钟的费用。在斯坦斯特德，在出发地外的“Express Set Down”处，10 英镑可以购买更慷慨的 15 分钟。伦敦城市机场今年才开始让人们付费，按每分钟计算是最贵的。五分钟收费 8 英镑，每分钟 1.60 英镑。相比之下，根据 RAC 的研究，欧盟排名前 10 的机场均不收取送机费。RAC 承认，英国机场通常会在距离航站楼较远的地点提供其他送机选择，有时会提供免费班车。但汽车组织的高级政策官员罗德·丹尼斯 (Rod Dennis) 表示，“拥有大量观众不应成为收取这些‘告别费’的借口——尤其是“当他们与世界各地主要机场的运营方式完全不一致时。”他说，司机需要特别小心无障碍的“现在停车，稍后付款”下客区，因为如果他们未能在一定时间内在线或通过电话付款，他们可能会面临停车费通知。图片来源，盖蒂图片社图片说明，丹麦奥尔堡机场航站楼前的停车场第一个小时免费。当交通部长批准机场修建第二条跑道的计划时，其中一项要求是让 54% 的乘客使用公共交通。希思罗机场可能还必须采取行动，阻止人们开车前往第三条跑道。伦敦盖特威克机场的一位发言人表示，下车收费旨在抑制“亲吻和飞翔”旅行，数据表明，这“影响乘客寻求其他选择”。机场连接 120他们说，伦敦和东南部的火车站有 8 条当地巴士路线 24/7 运行。乘客仍然可以在长期停车场免费下车，并有免费班车前往航站楼，蓝徽章持有者可以免除费用。他们说，英国机场局的发言人表示，在收取下车费的地方，“有必要管理拥堵、当地社区的交通和空气污染问题以及我们的气候”他们说：“这些是政府和地方当局必须管理的问题。”但商务旅行协会首席执行官克莱夫·拉滕表示，收费增加了企业的成本。“首先是环境原因，然后是拥堵管理。现在显然是为了抵消税收上涨。”出发前付钱给他们。蓝色徽章持有者有时可以豁免。寻找更便宜或免费的替代方案，例如稍远的停车场，那里有前往航站楼的班车。研究可能的公共交通替代方案。留意时间；尽快告别，以避免超过停留限制。检查出租车将在哪里下车以及他们的票价中是否包含该费用。相关主题航空旅行盖特威克机场伦敦斯坦斯特德机场旅行个人理财

title: IBM 在盈利预警后下调全年预期
content:
IBM 降低了 2026 年的预测，尽管该公司上周发布了盈利预警，但其盈利仍低于分析师周三的预期。 IBM 表示，其目标是通过提高生产率，将全年税前利润率扩大约 1 个百分点。

该公司相对于 LSEG 共识的表现如下：

根据一份声明，IBM 本季度收入同比增长 1%。净利润为 21.7 亿美元，即每股 2.30 美元，低于一年前的 21.9 亿美元，即每股 2.36 美元。调整后的收益不包括与收购相关的调整。

管理层预计 2026 年按固定汇率计算收入增长 4% 至 5%。就在 4 月份，IBM 一直在寻求按固定汇率计算超过 5% 的增长。该公司重申今年自由现金流将增加 10 亿美元的预期。

在 IBM 公布第二季度初步业绩后，分析师下调了预期。在给投资者的一封信中，首席执行官 Arvind Krishna 指出，由于各组织在预期价格上涨之前急于购买硬件，Z 大型计算机和交易处理软件的销售业绩低于计划。该股下跌 25%，创下有记录以来的最大单日跌幅。

IBM 周三披露的收入和调整后每股收益数据与一周前发布的数据一致。

截至周三收盘，IBM 股价 2026 年迄今已下跌 30%，而标准普尔 500 指数上涨约 10%。

IBM 表示，其高利润软件部门的利润为 7.76 美元第二季度营收增长5%。咨询收入为 53.3 亿美元，持平。基础设施收入为 38.4 亿美元，下降 7%，其中 Z 大型机收入下降 42%。

本季度，IBM 表示签署了建立美国量子芯片代工厂的意向书。它还推出了 Bobartificial 智能编码工具，该工具依赖于混合生成模型，已获得超过 80,000 名员工的采用。

“IBM 正在通过利用人工智能扩展软件开发、提高销售和营销组织的效率以及优化供应链来提高生产力，该公司在周三的声明中表示。“这些努力有助于提高利润和自由现金流，并增强公司抓住重大增长机会的能力。”

观看：Melius 的 Ben Reitzes 探讨 IBM 的下一步发展

title: 美国与沙特阿拉伯签署核电协议
content:
美国周三与沙特阿拉伯签署了一项协议，在该国开发民用核计划。

能源部表示，该协议将为长达数十年、价值数十亿美元的伙伴关系奠定法律基础。该部门表示，它将为美国公司提供参与沙特阿拉伯核能计划的机会，从而促进美国在该领域的技术出口。

能源部长克里斯·赖特和沙特能源部长阿卜杜勒阿齐兹·本·萨勒曼亲王还签署了一项关于核保障的双边协议。

赖特表示，该协议将“维护核安全和防扩散的最高标准，同时依靠世界上最好的核技术和科学家”。美国

该部门表示，该交易将提交国会审查。美国与外国在民用核电方面的合作需要根据《原子能法》进行此类审查。

民主党议员对该协议对中东的影响表示担忧。美国和沙特阿拉伯在华盛顿轰炸伊朗之际签署了该协议，以迫使德黑兰放弃其核野心。

康涅狄格州参议员克里斯·墨菲在社交媒体帖子中表示，“该协议将引发该地区的核竞赛，进一步抑制伊朗限制自己的核计划。”

西屋电气首席执行官丹·萨姆纳将该协议描述为“具有里程碑意义的一步”。萨姆纳在一份声明中告诉 CNBC，这将加强能源安全并扩大美国工业的机会。

西屋电气是大型反应堆 AP1000 的开发商，该反应堆被视为扩大核能的关键技术。它的所有者是加拿大铀矿商 CamecoandBrookfield，该公司是能源基础设施的主要投资者。

提供核燃料市场信息的 UxC 总裁 Jonathan Hinze 表示，该协议将允许西屋公司、Bechtel、BWXT、Centrus 和其他公司向沙特阿拉伯出售敏感核设备。

“如果没有这种核贸易协议，美国核公司将继续被排除在外。市场，”欣泽说。 “沙特可能会选择法国、俄罗斯或中国等其他国家的核技术和供应。”

沙特目前几乎所有国内能源都来自化石燃料。据国际能源署称，该国近 60% 的电力来自天然气，约 40% 来自石油。

它是 OPEC 最大的石油生产国。

title: 分析：随着天然气和柴油价格攀升，伊朗战争能源冲击打击美国经济
content:
The U.S.economy is more vulnerablethan ever to theproblems in energy marketscaused bythe Iran war. The economy has been remarkably resilient, but the buffers that protected Americans from earlier price spikes are wearing thin.

Bottom line: The war will erode Americans'standard of livingthis summer even if the actual fighting stays more contained than during the first phase of the conflict.

FromPresident Donald Trump'sperspective, having kicked off a return to direct conflict with Iran, there is now little he can do to shield Americans from the economic fallout.

The White House says the president has been honest with the American people and that prices will fall soon. "As the U.S. military degrades the terrorist Iranian regime's ability to attack commercial vessels and disrupt the free flow of energy through the Strait of Hormuz, oil and gas prices will plummet back to pre-conflict levels," Taylor Rogers, a White House spokeswoman, said in an email.

Anyone who has filled up at the pump in recent days has already felt the pain. The national average price at the pump was $4.06 a gallon on Wednesday, up 4.4% from $3.89 a week ago,according to AAA.

That's painful. But for the full economic effect, watch diesel, "because of course that is the lifeblood of the U.S. economy," said Christian Lawrence, head of Americas and energy market strategy at Rabobank.

The U.S. Energy Information Administration'sbenchmarkfor diesel jumped nearly 34 cents last week to $5.13 a gallon, the biggest weekly climb since the first week of the war in March. Those figures are used to determine fuel surcharges that airlines and other companies impose on customers, which can push price hikes out to the rest of the economy.

We've been here before, of course. The start of the war in March sawoil prices spikeand gas and diesel prices follow, only to fall when the shooting paused a few weeks later. With periodic reports that the U.S. and Iran are trying to hash out a new pause in the fighting, it might seem like a return to lower prices is only a presidential Truth Social post away.

Unfortunately, that's where things have changed, particularly in economically vital diesel.

"There's a bit of an asymmetric relationship there in the sense that, if oil goes up, then diesel prices are going up," Lawrence said. "If oil goes down, diesel prices might come off a little bit, but they're still going to be much higher."

Problems have been accumulating in the refining sector that turns raw crude oil into the distilled products that actually get pumped into cars and trucks. U.S. refineries are at 96.1% of their capacity, the EIA said Wednesday.

If refineries could produce more at that level, they would. U.S. refineries kicked into overdrive when the war started to help produce jet fuel and other products for European markets that suddenly found themselves cut off from their suppliers in the Middle East.

Inventories that were run down early in the war have yet to be replenished amid summer demand. The EIA reported last week that storage at the crucial delivery point of Cushing, Oklahoma, has since early June been at so-calledtank bottom, the level at which remaining liquids can't physically be drawn out.

The Strategic Petroleum Reserve isdown to 311 million barrels, its lowest level since March 1983, according to EIA data.

Iran isn't the only war still raging, either. Ukraine hit 24 out of Russia's 34 largest refineries in the past three months, analysts with BofA Global Research note. Russia has switched from a supplier of diesel and other products to an importer, just as China is trying to restock its own inventories.

More oil is getting through the Strait of Hormuz now than during the teeth of the crisis in March, according to theInternational Energy Agency, even asship attacks in the waterwaycontinue to be a risk.But that oil doesn't help anyone until it's turned into something useful for the global economy. That makes benchmark prices like Brent crude, at $94 a barrel midday Wednesday, less important as an indicator than the retail prices consumers actually pay.

None of this amounts to an immediate economic crisis, but it adds to the affordability pressures that have been weighing on Americans for years. Inflation data delivered a happy surprise last week when the consumer price index came in at abetter-than-expected 3.5%for June. But the reprieve is likely temporary. Higher fuel prices will eat into wage gains and force Americans to dip deeper into their savings.

TheCNBC All-America Economic Surveyreleased last week found 37% of U.S. voters said they are using credit cards more often to pay for things due to higher food and gas prices. That's a 6% increase since April as the war has dragged on.

The administration has tried to stanch the bleeding. It directed a massive release of oil from the SPR, eased restrictions on which ships can carry fuel and other commodities, and reduced sanctions on Russian and Iranian oil. All of these measures are arguably already priced into the market, however, and it isn't clear there are more levers the administration can pull in the short term.

A lasting end to the conflict would lower oil prices, but gas and diesel may be stuck high at least through Labor Day, when the end of summer has fewer people on the roads. The surge in demand will eventually prompt more refinery construction.

"But this takes time. There is no short-term solution," Lawrence said.

title: OpenAI 网络模型突破训练环境，破解 Hugging Face
content:
OpenAI 表示，其人工智能模型是一场“前所未有的网络事件”的幕后黑手，该事件影响了开源开发者平台 Hugging Face，令整个行业的研究人员感到不安。

该公司表示，其模型组合 GPT-5.6 Soland 尚未发布的功能更强大的模型逃脱了沙盒测试环境，访问了互联网并利用漏洞访问了 Hugging Face 的OpenAI 在周二的博客文章中表示，该模型试图找到可用于在评估中作弊的信息，并且成功了。两家公司都在积极调查这一事件。

Hugging Face 透露，它正在调查上周的一起安全事件，并在当时的一份新闻稿中表示，该事件是独一无二的，因为它是“由自主 AI 代理系统端到端驱动的”。

“我们在过去 24 小时内与 @OpenAI 团队密切合作（谢谢！），我们坚信他们没有恶意，”Hugging Face 首席执行官 Clément Delangue 写道。周二在 X 上发表的一篇文章。 “这一切都是自主发生的，真是令人兴奋！”

自从 OpenAI 的竞争对手 Anthropic 四月份发布了名为 Claude Mythos Preview 的强大产品以来，华尔街和美国政府一直关注人工智能模型快速发展的网络能力。 OpenAI 于 5 月推出了自己的网络产品，随后于 6 月推出了 GPT-5.6 Sol，将其描述为“迄今为止最强大的网络安全模型。”

两家公司都对先进网络模型的风险发出了警告，并采取措施限制其对选定的公司集团和政府机构的可用性。

投资银行公司 Perella Weinberg 的咨询合伙人沃尔特·艾萨克森 (Walter Isaacson) 周三表示，他认为 Hugging Face 事件“确实是一个严重的问题”。令人恐惧”，尽管他认为自己是人工智能乐观主义者。

他在 CNBC 的“Squawk Box”节目中表示：“这是第一件让我完全害怕的事情。”

Yoshua Bengio，一位领先的人工智能研究员，荣获著名的 A.M. 2018 年图灵奖得主 Xon 周三在一篇文章中写道，这一事件“令人深感担忧”。他表示，几个月来，特工们在受控测试中表现出了作弊的意愿，但“这个现实世界的案例应该敲响警钟。”

“继续当前的人工智能发展轨迹，可能会导致自主网络攻击的具体案例以及其他错误和危险的人工智能行为的高风险事件增加，”本吉奥说。 “我们迫切需要采取行动来防止这些情况发生，而不是试图在事后消除损害。

OpenAI 周二表示，人工智能正在加速漏洞的发现和利用，这意味着模型的安全性和安全性需要跟上。

该公司表示：“我们正在加强模型开发过程中使用的遏制、监控、访问控制和评估实践。”

观看：OpenAI 董事长布雷特·泰勒 (Bret Taylor) 谈人工智能代币经济学、代币效率

title: 公司表示，伦敦劳合社前老板的关系违反了规定
content: 图片来源，Bloomberg via Getty Images 图片说明，伦敦劳合社前首席执行官约翰·尼尔撰文：Mitchell Labiak 商业记者发布 2026 年 7 月 22 日，英国夏令时 16:47 5 小时前更新保险市场经过内部调查后表示，伦敦劳合社前老板与另一位董事的密切关系违反了合规规定。前首席执行官约翰·尼尔和前公司事务总监该公司表示，丽贝卡·克莱门特的关系“非常亲密……可以被视为造成了明显的利益冲突”。劳合社表示，两人没有披露他们的关系，违反了合规规则，但没有发现确凿的证据表明他们在劳合社期间有过恋爱关系。尼尔表示，各方现在可以继续前进。克莱门特的律师表示，她正在考虑采取法律行动。克莱门特的律师补充道：“丽贝卡对劳埃德在调查过程中的行为感到非常失望，调查的性质和时间长度给她带来了不必要的压力，并且相对于‘调查结果’而言，她的名誉受到了重大损害。”克莱门特的律师补充道，“劳埃德没有发现任何与约翰·尼尔有不正当关系的证据，也没有发现任何她晋升过程中失败的证据，她对此并不感到惊讶。她也配合了调查“然而，劳合社仍然选择以‘感知’为借口对丽贝卡进行指控，其来源是谣言、八卦和影射。”尼尔说：“我很高兴，但一点也不感到惊讶，调查发现不存在不适当的关系。”“我本来希望在就核心问题得出结论时花费更少的时间和资源，事实上，这一点是毫无疑问的。“我对其他调查结果和其他调查结果感到失望劳合社周三表示，它于 2023 年 11 月首次收到“某些举报”，但没有采取行动。该公司表示，其董事长查尔斯·罗克斯堡爵士 (Sir Charles Roxburgh) 认为这是治理失败，并于 2025 年 10 月向金融行为监管局 (FCA) 通报了此事。劳合社表示，无法透露这些指控的性质或涉案人员的身份。2025 年 11 月，劳合社表示，查尔斯爵士意识到“与尼尔和克莱门特之间所谓的个人关系有关的新信息”，并“立即对此事展开了扩大调查”。劳合社表示，由于尼尔和克莱门特都离开了公司，并且拒绝回答问题，因此调查受到阻碍，但劳合社表示，在调查中采访了近 40 名证人。该公司补充说，在整个过程中，它一直向 FCA 通报情况。查尔斯爵士周三表示：“基于根据这次调查的结果，我们得出的结论是，前首席执行官的行为大大低于他的预期标准。”他补充说，调查“发现了治理标准和后续流程的严重缺陷，最令人担忧的是举报报告的处理。”这些都是严重的失败，绝不允许发生。“劳合社作为伦敦金融城机构的历史可以追溯到 300 多年前，首次有记录的提及出现在 1688 年。相关话题公司伦敦劳合社更多关于这个故事城市公司对奴隶制联系“深表歉意”发布于 2023 年 11 月 8 日英国石油公司主席突然被免职背后的“欺凌”和“霸道”行为发布于 5 月 27 日发布

title: 特朗普发誓，如果伊朗向霍尔木兹海峡的船只开枪，他将轰炸伊朗的桥梁和发电厂
content:
PresidentDonald Trumpon Wednesday vowed that the U.S. will blow up an Iranian bridge or power plant — including those in the country's capital city of Tehran — every timeIranshoots at a ship in theStrait of Hormuz.

The threat, delivered via aTruth Social post, ratchets up hostilities between the U.S. and the Islamic Republic that were already inflamed following the collapse of their ceasefire and the recent deaths of three American service members, whose remains Trump was scheduled to receive later Wednesday.

"From this point forward, any time the Islamic Republic of Iran shoots at a ship in the Strait of Hormuz, whether it be by Missile, Rocket, Drone, or any other device or weapon, the United States will bomb and destroy ONE BRIDGE OR POWER PLANT," Trump's post read.

The potential targets include "those located next to, or in, the Capital City of Tehran," Trump specified.

Iran responded by threatening to strike U.S.-linked infrastructure and energy facilities across the region if Washington carries out Trump's threat.

"If the Americans target a bridge or a power plant in Iran, Iran will, in turn, strike infrastructure and bridges in the region, including energy facilities where the United States has interests," an unnamed Iranian military source told the Tasnim News Agency, an Iranian state-run media outlet, which posted on Telegram.

The source said Iran remained determined to control passage through the Strait of Hormuz, saying ships would be allowed to pass safely only if their transit was coordinated with Tehran and conducted under Iranian arrangements, the source said.

Trump has previously threatened to attack Iran's electrical plants, water facilities and other infrastructure. Experts have warned that such attacks could constitutewar crimesunder international law.

Earlier Wednesday, Secretary of State Marco Rubio accused Iran of not being "serious" about making a deal with the U.S., while reiterating that Washington was "committed to diplomacy" in the Middle East.

His comments come as U.S. Central Commandhit Iranian targetsfor an 11th straight night as it continues to try to clear the Hormuz Strait, the vital oil shipping route that has become Tehran's key source of leverage in the war.

Centcomsaidit had hit military operations centers, drone storage facilities and military logistics infrastructure.

Speaking at a meeting of foreign ministers of the Association of Southeast Asian Nations in the Philippines, Rubio said a key sticking point between Teheran and Washington was that Iran "demands the right" to control traffic in the Strait of Hormuz, a right it does not have under international law.

"If we create a precedent in the Middle East, where a nation state can decide that they are going to control an international waterway, charge a toll, and if you don't pay them, blow up your ships, we have created a very dangerous precedent, which will repeat itself in other parts of the world, including in this region," Rubio warned.

He appeared to make a veiled reference to China, saying that "of all the regions in the world, the one I don't need to teach about the importance of freedom of navigation in international waterways, it is this region."

In separate comments made later in the day, Rubio said Washington remains willing to negotiate an end to the war.

"The U.S. would love to reach a diplomatic settlement, we'd love to reach an agreement if it were possible with Iran … where they say we'll no longer sponsor terrorism and we will not pursue a nuclear weapon or the things that you need for a nuclear weapon," he said.

But Rubio also said that "right now they don't seem to be serious" about making a deal, adding that Tehran had made commitments in last month's memorandum of understanding "and within two weeks violated it."

The U.S. Navy has in the past conducted"freedom of navigation"operations around disputed islands in the South China Sea, where several Southeast Asian countries have competing claims with China, whose "nine-dash line" claims almost all of the sea as its waters.

That is despite a 2016 ruling by the Permanent Court of Arbitration that said that China's claims has no basis under international law. China has refused to recognize that outcome.

Oil pricesjumpedon Wednesday morning. International benchmarkBrent crudefutures with September delivery rose 3.7% to $94.33 per barrel, while U.S.West Texas Intermediatefutures with September delivery advanced 3.5% to $87.28.

The U.S. and Iran had signed aninterim memorandum of understandingon June 17 that halted military operations, called for the reopening of Hormuz, and established a 60-day window for negotiating a permanent agreement, including terms covering Iran's nuclear program. But the document left the future management of the Strait of Hormuz unresolved.

Iraninterpreted the textas recognizing a role for Tehran in managing traffic through the strait and potentially charging for maritime services after an initial 60-day period, but the U.S. maintained that the waterway must remain open without compulsory Iranian fees or restrictions.

Iran and Oman — which also borders the Hormuz Strait — said in ajoint June 23 statementthat "All arrangements related to the Strait of Hormuz must fully respect the sovereignty and sovereign rights of the two Coastal States of the Strait."

The strikes on Iran are "consequences" for Tehran not keeping its word, Rubio said, adding "If these consequences did not exist, then today we would be living in a world where the significant percentage of the energy supply of this region and 20% of the energy supply of the world is being held hostage by a nation state."

—CNBC's Luke Fountaincontributed to this report.

title: Reddit 股价下跌，因有报道称其可能不会续签 Google AI 内容交易
content:
《华尔街日报》报道称，Reddit 的股价周三下跌 9%，此前该公司已讨论关闭谷歌对其用于人工智能用途的内容的访问。

Reddit 股价今年迄今已下跌约 19%。

两家公司于 2024 年达成协议，允许这家搜索巨头根据 Reddit 的内容训练其人工智能模型。但据《华尔街日报》援引知情人士的消息称，随着谷歌的人工智能总结减少了搜索结果页面的网站流量，Reddit 正在重新考虑这笔交易的好处。

其他网站上也可以看到搜索流量趋势，Politico 的 Google 流量下降了 23%，CNN 下降了约 25%，Business Insider 的流量在 2025 年 6 月至 2025 年 6 月之间下降了 85% 以上。据《华尔街日报》报道，2026 年。

CNBC 已联系 Reddit 和 Google 征求意见。

据《华尔街日报》报道，Reddit 与 Google 每年 6000 万美元的协议即将结束，两家公司正在就可能续签合作伙伴关系进行谈判。

Reddit 还与 OpenAI 建立了类似的合作伙伴关系，允许 ChatGPT 制造商根据其内容训练其人工智能模型，作为Reddit 的数据许可业务帮助推动了上季度的盈利。

第一季度，该公司的收入同比增长 69%，超出了分析师的预期，每股收益为 1.01 美元，而预期为 58 美分，收入为 6.63 亿美元，而预期为 6.11 亿美元。

“我们与 Google 和 OpenAI 都建立了重要的合作伙伴关系，”Reddit 首席执行官霍夫曼在谈到收益时向分析师表示。打电话。 “这些对我们来说非常有意义，我认为这是相互的。我们继续重视这些。”

该公司将于 7 月 30 日报告第二季度收益。

title: 海军组织称，胡塞武装部署导弹和无人机攻击红海南部的船只
content:
美国海军领导的一个海上安全组织警告称，伊朗在也门的胡塞盟友已经完成了攻击通过红海南部入口的船只的准备工作。

“接近该组织的消息人士称，胡塞武装已经完成了攻击航运的准备工作，包括在曼德海峡附近部署导弹和无人机，”联合海事信息中心（JMIC）在一份通知中告诉水手们。星期二。该中心总部位于巴林，为中东的商船提供安全更新。

胡塞武装在美伊战争期间多次威胁要关闭曼德海峡。该海峡将红海与亚丁湾、阿拉伯海和全球市场连接起来。

武装分子周一宣布对沙特阿拉伯实施海上禁运，威胁要切断该王国通过红海和曼德海峡的石油出口。随着伊朗袭击霍尔木兹海峡的油轮，这些出口已成为全球石油市场的重要安全阀。

利雅得每天通过管道将数百万桶石油转移到红海的延布码头。根据 Kpler 数据，6 月份该码头通过曼德海峡的出口量激增至每天 350 万桶，而去年同期仅为 24 万桶。

JMIC 周二表示，红海南部和曼德海峡的商业运输仍在继续，过去两天没有发生袭击事件。 JMIC 表示，对该地区船只的威胁仍然“中等”。

JMIC 表示，“商业路线或过境模式没有发生变化，船只继续使用既定航线，没有受到干扰。”

然而，船舶跟踪公司 Kpler 观察到，与周一胡塞武装宣布对沙特实施禁运的周一相比，周二通过曼德海峡的交通量下降了 34%。

沙特有四艘油轮据海事情报公司 Windward 称，货物于周二抵达曼德海峡之前掉头。该公司表示，这些船只载有 380 万桶原油、柴油和石脑油。

与此同时，伊朗加大了对霍尔木兹油轮的攻击力度，试图迫使船只通过其领海通过海峡。本周，三艘原油、成品油和化学品油轮在阿曼和阿拉伯联合酋长国海岸遭到袭击。

据联合国机构国际海事组织称，自 7 月 6 日以来，霍尔木兹及其周边地区已有十几艘船只遭到袭击，导致两名海员死亡、十几人受伤。

title: 特朗普的仿制药关税：对药品价格、制造商和供应意味着什么
content:
PresidentDonald Trump'sproposed tariffs on imported generic medicineshave raised fresh questions about whether low-cost drugmakers can shift production to the U.S. before duties of as much as 200% take effect.

Trump said Tuesdaythat imported generic medicines would face no tariffs for two years before duties rise to 100% for one year and then 200%, giving manufacturers time to invest in U.S. production.

The proposal targets a growing global industry, currently worth nearly $500 billion.

While the administration says tariffs will help bring pharmaceutical manufacturing back to the U.S., the generic drug industry argues that longstanding structural challenges, not just trade policy, limit domestic production.

"We need to understand more the specifics of the policy, but the generics industry is committed to pursuing policies that support and stabilize both the industry and the access necessary to ensure patients have reliable options for affordable medicines," John Murphy III, president and CEO of the Association for Accessible Medicines, said in a statement to CNBC.

Murphy said the industry has expanded its U.S. footprint across the supply chain over the past two years, but argued that problems with purchasing and reimbursement for many generic medicines continue to discourage further domestic manufacturing.

Makers of patented medicines and generic drug manufacturers have very different business models, which means they are likely to see varying impacts from potential tariffs.

Brand-name drugmakers typically spend years developing costly, new medicines and, once approved, benefit from exclusivity that allows them to sell those drugs without direct competition for a limited period of time. Those companies generally have higher margins and greater flexibility to absorb higher manufacturing costs.

By contrast, generic manufacturers enter the market after patents expire and often compete against other companies selling identical versions of the same medicine, competing on price, manufacturing efficiency, and scale.

That means even relatively small increases in manufacturing costs can have an outsized impact on profitability.

Generic medicines account for about90% of prescriptions in the U.S., but a relatively small share of overall drug spending due to their lower prices.

Industry representatives say many manufacturers have limited room to absorb tariffs as high as 100% or 200%.

Manufacturers facing higher costs would have limited options: absorb the tariffs, pass them on to customers, invest in shifting production to the U.S. over time or stop selling products that become uneconomic.

Building a domestic generic drug manufacturing ecosystem takes a minimum of four to five years, according to Namit Joshi, chairman of India's Pharmaceuticals Export Promotion Council (Pharmexcil), suggesting Trump's two-year implementation period may not be enough to meaningfully onshore production.

Indian manufacturers also operate on thin margins, he said. "We can only transfer that tariff. Or we can withdraw from the market,"Joshi told Indian news agency ANI.

Independent pharmaceutical consultant Salil Kallianpur said the economics are particularly challenging for commodity generic manufacturers that export to the U.S.

If manufacturing costs rise sharply, companies may have to absorb part of the increase, pass it on to customers, invest in moving production over time or stop selling products that are no longer commercially viable.

"A 100-200% tariff on a product with single-digit margins is a market-exit notice," Kallianpur told CNBC over email.

If this holds as written, the effect is a likely split where companies with existing U.S. manufacturing footprint or complex, specialty portfolios can adapt, while pure-volume commodity exporters with no U.S. presence don't have an obvious answer, he said.

It remains unclear whether the proposed tariffs would ultimately increase medicine prices because much depends on how the policy is implemented and how manufacturers respond.

The administration argues the tariffs will encourage companies to manufacture more medicines in the U.S., strengthening domestic supply chains over the longer term.

Industry representatives, meanwhile, say tariffs could place additional pressure on an industry where prices are already driven down by intense competition.

Many generic medicines sold in the U.S. are manufactured in India, while China supplies many of the active pharmaceutical ingredients used to make finished drugs. Those supply chains have developed over decades around lower production costs.

Murphy said the generic industry supports expanding U.S. manufacturing but believes broader policy changes are also needed.

"Our industry has several legislative and regulatory solutions to address the market deficiencies and we look forward to dialogue with the Administration and with Congress to pursue solutions that restore the generics industry to growth and to prioritize its place as a critical national security asset here in the U.S.," he said.

The impact is also likely to vary significantly by company.

Analysts at Jefferies and Citi say manufacturers with substantial U.S. production, such asAmphastar Pharmaceuticals,ANI Pharmaceuticals,Hikma, andFresenius Kabi, appear better positioned if the tariffs are implemented largely as proposed.

Companies includingTeva,ViatrisandApotexhave a greater exposure as they manufacture a larger share of products sold in the U.S. overseas, although analysts caution that much depends on the final policy.

One key unanswered question is whether the tariffs would apply only to imported finished medicines or also to drugs manufactured in the U.S. using imported active pharmaceutical ingredients.

Sandoz, one of the world's largest generic drugmakers, told CNBC it was too early to assess the proposal because "further details on the implementation and scope of the measure are still required."

The Swiss company declined to comment on whether the announcement could affect its manufacturing footprint or future investment plans.

For now, Kallianpur said investors appear to be treating the two-year implementation period as breathing room rather than an immediate disruption.

Generic drugmakers had broadly expected the Trump administration to extend its pharmaceutical tariff strategy to generics after months ofsimilar proposals targeting branded medicines, he added.

"What's new here isn't the direction, it's the specificity," he said, pointing to the timeline of two tariff-free years followed by duties of 100% and then 200%.

Much now depends on how the administration defines domestic manufacturing and implements the policy.

If the generic tariff follows the framework previously outlined for branded medicines, Kallianpur said, companies may only need to demonstrate that U.S. manufacturing projects are underway rather than fully operational before the deadline.

That distinction could shape how manufacturers respond over the next two years, and whether Trump's proposal leads to a significant expansion of U.S. drug production or simply a wave of announcements for new factories before the tariffs take effect.

title: 参议员怀登提出限制特朗普关税权力的法案
content:
参议员。俄勒冈州民主党人罗恩·怀登 (Ron Wyden) 周三提出了一项法案，该法案将限制唐纳德·特朗普 (Donald Trump) 总统的宽泛关税权力，取消其设定进口关税的一些法定工具，并赋予国会对剩下的工具更大的发言权。

在抨击特朗普宣布对多种加拿大商品征收 50% 报复性关税的第二天，怀登公布了这项新立法，并援引了一项近百年历史的贸易法，而该法很少（如果有的话）被实施。参议院财政委员会民主党领袖怀登在一份声明中表示：“唐纳德·特朗普滥用了他掌握的所有贸易权力，他现在正在挖掘大萧条时期的一项法律，再次对我们最亲密的盟友和贸易伙伴之一的产品征收巨额单方面关税。”“这是又一次的整顿，将提高美国人、他们的家庭和全国各地小企业的生活成本。”怀登表示，并补充说，他将提出一项法案，“让国会重新掌握主导权。”

该法案很可能在国会获得通过，因为共和党在参众两院均占多数。即使该法案确实获得通过，特朗普也可以否决该法案。

美国宪法赋予国会征收关税的权力，但随着时间的推移，立法部门已授予总统对外国商品征收关税的重大权力。怀登的法案被称为“2026 年国会贸易权力改革法案”，旨在扭转这一趋势。

该法案要求特朗普获得国会批准根据三个权力机构（即第 301、201 和 232 条）提出的关税提议。

1974 年《贸易法》第 301 条授权行政部门对被认为不公平的对外贸易行为征收关税。美国

根据同一法律第 201 条，如果美国国际贸易委员会发现进口激增严重威胁国内产业，总统可以征收关税。

1962 年贸易扩张法案第 232 条允许总统以国家安全为由征收关税。

怀登的法案还将取消参议员所说的两个关税当局“过时了。”

它们是：1974年贸易法第122条，该条赋予总统在涉及国际支付问题的事项上征收关税的权力； 1930 年《关税法》第 338 条规定，总统可以对被发现歧视美国的国家的商品征收高达 50% 的关税。

该法案将设立一个国会“关税与贸易联合委员会”，要求总统向该委员会提交关税提案。该委员会将由来自参议院财政委员会和众议院筹款委员会各五名成员组成。

该委员会将有最多 30 天的时间审查总统的提案，并决定是否建议国会在一定时间内就联合决议进行投票。

该委员会还将加强对美国贸易代表办公室的监督，将其设为总统行政办公室之外的一个独立机构，并在总统行政办公室内设立监察长。

白宫没有立即回应 CNBC 对怀登法案发表评论的请求。

title: OpenAI 称其人工智能失控并发起“前所未有”的网络攻击
content: Image source,Getty ImagesImage caption,OpenAI is best known for its chatbot ChatGPT, which is used by hundreds of millions of people every weekByLaura CressTechnology reporterPublished22 July 2026, 11:21 BSTUpdated 3 hours agoOpenAI has revealed some of its most advanced AI models went rogue and hacked a start-up after it lost control of them during a security test.The ChatGPT-maker said its agent - an AI system which can operate alone after human instruction â was being tested in a controlled environment but, after finding weaknesses, was able to escape the test limits.They targeted Hugging Face, one of the world's largest hubs for sharing AI models, gaining access to some internal company systems.OpenAIsaid the incident was "unprecedented",external, and it was conducting an investigation alongside Hugging Face, whose boss Clement Delangue said in a post on X it was "mind-blowing that all of this happened autonomously"."The investigation is ongoing, and we'll share more learnings from what might be the first incident of its kind," Delangue added.A government spokesperson said the UK's AI Security Institute was studying the behaviour from the AI system seen in the incident and was continuing to work with OpenAI and other labs to improve safeguards.They said organisations should step up their cyber-defences by taking steps such as enrolling in the government-backed Cyber Essentials certification scheme.Insecure sandboxesGina Neff, head of the Minderoo Centre for Technology and Democracy at the University of Cambridge, told BBC Radio 4's Today programme that the security tests - called sandboxes - are "supposed to be secure environments where you can see what the models are capable of"."In this case, it looks like OpenAI didn't make a secure enough sandbox," she added.Instead, the agents created their own cyber-attack against the sandbox itself, finding a vulnerability which allowed them to escape the restrictions.Once outside, the AI identified Hugging Face as a likely source of the answers they were seeking in the test, and tried to gain access.Neil Lawrence, Professor of machine learning at Cambridge University, called it an "impressive feat", but cautioned it "falls well within the known capabilities of the current generation" of high-powered AI models.He pointed out that OpenAI is looking to list itself on the stock market, and faces intense pressure from rival firm Anthropic, which has made headlines withits own powerful AI tool, Mythos."OpenAI are now playing catch-up, they are trying to demonstrate their own systems' capabilities in cyber-security.""It shows us that OpenAI are not capable of safely deploying their own technology," he added.In itsinitial disclosure of the hack on 16 July,external, Hugging Face said it was still assessing whether any customer or partner data was affected and would contact affected parties if necessary.It said it has now closed the vulnerabilities highlighted by the incident and rebuilt the affected systems."Autonomous, AI-driven offensive tooling is no longer theoretical," it said."Defending an online platform now means treating the data and model surface as a first-class attack surface, and using AI on defence to keep pace."We will keep investing there, and keep sharing what we learn."'Sobering moment'The incident has prompted fresh questions about the capabilities of advanced AI systems and whether existing safeguards are sufficient as the technology becomes more powerful.Spencer Starkey, an executive at cyber-security firm SonicWall, told the BBC the incident made it clear organisations needed to "step up" their own defences and "treat cyber resilience as a core operational priority"."The uncomfortable truth is that too many organisations are still defending at human speed while adversaries are escalating to machine speed," he said.Meanwhile Travis Lelle, principal security engineer at cyber-security consulting firm Guidepoint Security, said the update marked a "sobering moment in cyber-security"."This highlights a known asymmetry," he said."Offensive agents are unconstrained, while the best defensive tools are locked behind guardrails that cannot understand context."But Jake Moore, global cyber-security advisor at ESET, said the announcement could also have a competitive dimension.He argued OpenAI may be seeking to highlight its own AI capabilities as rival Anthropic attracts growing attention for its Claude Mythos model."It does pose the question that OpenAI are potentially chasing the marketing dream of Anthropic of late," he said.It comes a week after Chinese AI start-up Moonshot unveiled Kimi K3 - a massive new artificial intelligence modelit said could rival top US firms.Apple sues OpenAI, its employees claiming theft of trade secretsPublished10 JulyWhat is Claude Mythos and what risks does it pose?Published17 AprilHow to stop AI agents going roguePublished26 August 2025Sign up for our Tech Decoded newsletterto follow the world's top tech stories and trends.Outside the UK? Sign up here.Related topicsArtificial intelligenceCyber-attacksCyber-security

title: 抵押贷款利率再次上升，但购房者看到了一些优势
content:
上周抵押贷款利率继续攀升，但购房者逐渐重返市场，或许是利用了竞争减少和价格下调的优势。根据抵押贷款银行家协会的季节性调整指数，这推动上周抵押贷款总需求较前一周上涨 1.9%。

贷款余额在 832,750 美元或以下的 30 年期固定利率抵押贷款的平均合同利率从 6.65% 增至 6.69%，贷款点数从 0.67 降至 0.62（包括贷款发起费）并支付 20% 的首付。这是自去年 8 月以来的最高利率。

因此，对每周利率变动高度敏感的再融资需求本周下降了 2%，仅比一年前同一周高出 7%。去年这个时候，30 年期固定贷款的平均水平仅高出 15 个基点。

购房抵押贷款申请本周增长 6%，同比增长 0.2%，基本持平。随着市场进入历史上最缓慢的夏季月份，潜在买家开始稍作休息。 CNBC 住房市场调查报告中的房地产经纪人似乎更愿意降价。

CNBC 的《与戴安娜·奥利克 (Diana Olick) 一起玩房地产》为房地产投资者提供了新的、不断变化的机会，每周发送到您的收件箱。

在此处订阅即可立即访问。

“许多市场不断增长的房屋库存正在支持更多的购买活动，”MBA 高级副总裁兼首席经济学家 Mike Fratantoni 表示。 “即将公布的数据显示，6 月通胀有所下降，但随着油价再次飙升，7 月数据中的改善似乎不太可能持续，因此抵押贷款利率可能会继续走高。”

根据《抵押新闻日报》的另一项调查，抵押贷款利率从本周开始甚至更高，与 5 月中旬以来的最后一个高点持平。与伊朗的战争的新升级掩盖了上周低于预期的通胀报告。

“对于那些希望分析简单的人来说，燃油价格足以解释这一走势。事实上，8 月汽油期货本周也刚刚触及 5 月 19 日的高点，与利率的往返走势完全一致，”《抵押新闻日报》首席运营官马修·格雷厄姆 (Matthew Graham) 写道。

title: 随着投资者准备获得更多收益，股票期货下跌，伊朗战争仍然是焦点：实时更新
content:
Stock futures fell early Wednesday as investors looked ahead to another busy day of corporate earnings, with several major technology and industrial companies set to report.

Futures tied to theDow Jones Industrial Averagedipped 24 points, or less than 0.1%.S&P 500 futuresandNasdaq-100 futureswere down 0.3% and 0.7%, respectively.

Earnings take center stage again on Wednesday, with reports due fromServiceNow,International Business Machines,Tesla,Texas Instruments,AlphabetandAT&T. Investors will be watching closely for updates on AI spending, cloud demand, corporate technology budgets and the outlook for the second half of the year.

Investors remain focused on whether strong demand for AI infrastructure and software will continue to justify elevated valuations across the technology sector as earnings season accelerates.

"Q2 earnings season is ramping up, but with most hyperscalers yet to report, the market is still waiting for the definitive read on how AI investment is being monetized and translated into future capital spending," said Julia Hermann, global market strategist at New York Life Investment Management.

The major U.S. averagessnapped a three-session losing streakon Tuesday, supported by better-than-expected earnings from blue-chip companies including3MandGeneral Motors, which helped ease concerns about the health of corporate America. Chipmakers also fueled the rally, with theVanEck Semiconductor ETF (SMH)climbing 4% as investors rotated back into artificial intelligence-related names.

Investor attention is also attuned to developments in the Middle East after U.S. Central Command carried out its 11th consecutive night of strikes against Iran.

Oil prices jumped in early trading after U.S. Secretary of State Marco Rubio told the ASEAN Foreign Ministers' meeting in the Philippines on Wednesday that Iran is "not serious about talks."

If they're serious, we're serious. If they're not, then we will do what is necessary to protect our interests and also the interests of our allies," he said.

Rubio added Wednesday that the strait — a critical shipping route for oil and other vital commodities — remains a sticking point in bilateral talks, alleging that Iran "demands the right" to control the waterway. Allowing this to happen would set "a very dangerous precedent" for the world, he argued.

Oilextended its recent rallyas escalating tensions in the Middle East raised concerns about potential supply disruptions. Shortly before 5 a.m. ET, global benchmarkBrent crude futuresfor July delivery were 3.5% higher at $94.20, paring earlier gains. Front-monthU.S. West Texas Intermediate crudefutures were up 3.8% to trade at $87.56.

As of Wednesday morning, money markets were pricing in a 24.1% chance of a rate hike from the Fed this month, and a 69% chance of at least a quarter-point hike in September, according to the CME's FedWatch tool.

U.S. Treasury yields inched higher on Wednesday as investors mapped escalating tensions across the Middle East and mulled prospects for Federal Reserve monetary policy in the months ahead.

The yield on the10-year U.S. Treasurynote — the key benchmark for U.S. government borrowing — was broadly flat at 4.626%.

The2-year Treasury noteyield, which more closely tracks short-term Federal Reserve interest rate policy, fell one basis point to 4.251%. The longer-dated30-year Treasury bondyield was flat at 5.132%.

Precious metals traded higher on Wednesday, with gold prices surging to a two-week high as the Middle East conflict continues to weigh on markets.

Gold futureswere last seen 1.17% higher, at $4,125.00, whilespot goldprices were trading at $4,120.32, a 1.08% rise.

Investors flocked to bullion — long regarded a safe-haven asset during bouts of volatility — as the U.S. launched strikes against Iran for the 11thconsecutive day.

Silver also advanced, withfuturesrising 1.26% to $59.86, asspot silverreached $59.52, an increase of 1.25%.

Other precious also edged up, with platinum gaining 1.36% to $1,659.80 and palladium trading 1.27% higher at $1303.00.

The pan-EuropeanStoxx 600was last seen trading 0.6% higher, erasing losses seen earlier in the trading session.

Mining, utilities and oil and gas stocks led gains, while tech stocks slumped, as investors pivoted to assets seen as safe during bouts of volatility.

Investors are assessing a slew of corporate earnings and developments in the Middle East, with fading hopes of a U.S.-Iran ceasefirepushing oil prices higherthis morning.

Shares of Airbus rose as much as 7% in morning trading after the aircraft manufacturer provided a business update on Tuesday after markets closed."Airbus disclosed a mid-term guidance that aligned well with current market expectations," said Deutsche Bank analyst Christophe Menard. "The positive surprise, however, came from the €5 billion three-year share buy-back."

Airbus now targets adjusted earnings before interest and tax of between 12 billion euros and 13 billion euros ($13.6 billion to $14.8 billion) in 2029, indicating commercial growth will not flatten out with wide-body aircraft and service growth in the cards.

Full-year 2026 guidance remains unchanged.

Oil prices were 4% higher on Wednesday morning following the 11th consecutive round of U.S. strikes against Iran overnight, as Secretary of State Marco Rubio said the Strait of Hormuz remains a sticking point between the two sides.

Shortly after 4 a.m. ET, global benchmarkBrent crude futuresfor July delivery were almost 4% higher at $94.23. Front-monthU.S. West Texas Intermediate crudefutures were up 3.8% to trade at $87.46.

Taiwan's Taiex climbed 1.34% to close at 44,825.78, led by technology stocks. AI server makerWiwynnand memory chip producerNanya Technologyboth surged 10%. TSMC however declined 0.41%

Mainland China's CSI 300 slipped 0.46% to 4,717.24.Hong Kong'sHang Seng Indexwas down 1% as of 3.17 a.m. ET.

EquinorChief Financial Officer Torgrim Reitan warned Wednesday that Europe is unlikely to reach its gas storage target ahead of winter, saying the region finds itself in a "very fragile" situation.

His comments come as energy market participants closely monitor a resumption inMiddle East hostilitiesand a steep fall in traffic through the strategically vital Strait of Hormuz.

"Even if, sort of, things normalize around Hormuz and we get back to normal trade flows, we do not believe that Europe will get to the target level of 80% storage fills before the winter," Reitan told CNBC's "Europe Early Edition" on Wednesday.

The Norwegian oil and gas major reported adjusted earnings before tax for the second quarter of$11.48 billion, in line with expectations and up sharply from$6.53 billionfrom a year ago.

The results were boosted by a surge in fossil fuel prices as the U.S.-Iran conflict continues to disrupt global energy supplies.

European shares opened in negative territory on Wednesday morning, as corporate earnings and the U.S.-Iran war remained in focus.

Shortly after the opening bell, the pan-EuropeanStoxx 600was almost 0.4% lower, with most sectors and all major bourses in the red.

Oil and gas stocks bucked the trend, rising as oil prices rallied amid a further escalation of fighting in the Middle East.

In a surprise move late on Monday,John Healeywas announced as newly minted Prime MinisterAndy Burnham's finance minister.

Just weeks earlier, Healey resigned from his role as defense minister, citing government unwillingness to spend sufficiently on security "at this time of rising threats."

As investors took Healey's new role as a sign that the government will commit to higher defense spending, analysts and market watchers told CNBC which stocks they were watching.

U.K. inflation cooled to 2.6% in the 12 months to June, slightly lower than expected.

Economists polled by Reuters had been expecting the annual inflation rate to reach 2.7% in June.

The U.K.'s inflation ratedropped to 2.8% in Apriland held at that level in May, with the government-regulated price cap on energy bills softening the full impact of the Iran war on the energy market.

The price cap isdueto rise by 13% later this summer, when energy costs will hit a 2-year high.

June's U.K. inflation print came in lower than the euro zone's 2.8% reading for June, and well below the U.S. inflation rate of 3.5% for June.

Shares of Samsung Electronics rose over 4% on Wednesday amid a broader rise in tech stocks in the region.

The company is also reportedly in talks to invest hundreds of millions of euros in artificial intelligence startup Mistral. The move is part of a funding round that could value the French company at about 20 billion euros ($22.8 billion), theFinancial Times reported, citing people familiar with the matter.

Shares of domestic rival SK Hynix were up over 5%.

Japanese chip stocks were also higher, withAdvantestclimbing 2.8% andTokyo Electronadding 1.8%.Renesas ElectronicsandSoftbank Groupadded over 6% and 1.1% respectively.

Oil prices edged higher on Wednesday as concerns over supply disruptions persisted after U.S. forces launched a fresh round of strikes on Iranian military targets.

Brent crude futures for September deliverygained 1.2% to $92.1 a barrel, whileU.S. West Texas Intermediatefutures added 1.09% to $85.26.

The renewed military exchanges come as mediators are reportedly seeking to revive diplomacy, having presented Washington and Tehran with a proposal for a 10-day ceasefire.
